name: Engine Co. No 28 Chocolate Bread Pudding
recipeIngredient: 6 cups French bread (crust removed and diced into 1/4 inch pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the cubed bread in a bowl. Add 2 cups of milk to absorb.
2. Heat the remaining 1/2 cup of milk over low heat and add chocolate. Stir occasionally until the chocolate melts.
3. Cream together butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time.
4. Stir in vanilla, cinnamon, and the chocolate mixture into the bread mixture. Let stand for 20 minutes.
5. Grease two 1-1/2 quart pudding molds or use two 8x4 inch loaf pans or eight-inch round or square cake pans, and line with parchment paper.
6. Pour the mixture into the molds and cover with foil. Tie the top with kitchen string to keep out any moisture from steam if the pan does not have a cover or lip large enough for crimping foil.
7. Steam for about 1 hour 15 minutes, or until a cake tester inserted near the center comes out clean. Remove the lid or foil and cool slightly on a rack.
8. Invert the mold onto a plate and remove parchment if used. Cool to room temperature, then wrap and refrigerate or serve after more than a few hours later.
9. To serve, cut the pudding into 12 wedges if using a round pan or into slices for loaf pans. Spoon Creme Anglaise onto a dessert dish and place a wedge or slab over the sauce. Alternatively, lace the sauce over the pudding. Garnish with berries, sprigs of mint, or a dusting of powdered sugar.
